overview: Root cause: `src/controllers/index.js` registers all controller sub-modules in the `Controllers` export object, but the `well-known` controller module is missing from that registration. The route wiring in `src/routes/index.js` and the route handler in `src/routes/well-known.js` are already in place, but the controller is never exported, so any call to the well-known controller functions fails at runtime. Fix: add `Controllers.wellKnown = require('./well-known')` to `src/controllers/index.js` alongside the other single-file controller registrations. No other files need changes.

edits.change_rationale: exact_code_region: the Controllers export object in src/controllers/index.js registers every sub-controller module (e.g. `Controllers.categories = require('./categories')`, `Controllers.uploads = require('./uploads')`, etc.). The well-known controller module at src/controllers/well-known.js is fully implemented but absent from this export, which is why the /.well-known/webfinger and /.well-known/change-password routes (already wired in src/routes/index.js) fail. Adding the require line here is the only missing piece. No co-edit dependencies.
